serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single raw anchovy weighs approximately 10–15 g; a standard serving of fresh anchovy as part of a meal is approximately 80–100 g (6–8 small fish).

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 131 kcal, protein 20.35 g, fat 4.84 g, carbohydrate 0.0 g, sugar 0.0 g, sodium 104 mg, dietary fibre 0.0 g, potassium 383 mg, calcium 147 mg, iron 3.25 mg, cholesterol 60 mg) sourced directly from HPB Singapore nutrient composition database entry for Anchovy, raw (cr_id F03010044), an approved primary regional source, at confidence 0.95. Fatty acid disaggregation (saturated_fat_g 1.17, monounsaturated_fat_g 1.24, polyunsaturated_fat_g 1.64) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for Anchovy, raw (FDC ID 175159 / NDB 15001), an approved secondary global source, as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry. The USDA SR Legacy total fat (4.84 g) is consistent with the HPB value, confirming compatibility.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (consulted; not applicable for zero-carbohydrate foods)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Anchovy, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by HPB Singapore nutrient composition data. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted; glycemic index testing is not performed on foods with negligible carbohydrate content as there is no meaningful glycaemic response. Per Veritas protocol for zero-carbohydrate foods (pure proteins and fats), glycemic_index is set to null, gi_category to not_applicable, and glycemic_load_per_serving to 0.0. This is consistent with the treatment of all fish and seafood with negligible carbohydrates in the Veritas scoring model.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (104 mg/100 g), high potassium (383 mg/100 g), favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio consistent with DASH diet principles
hypertension.guidance: Raw anchovy is suitable for hypertension management due to its low sodium content (104 mg/100 g) and high potassium (383 mg/100 g); however, patients must strictly avoid canned, salted, or fermented anchovy products which can contain 3000–4000 mg sodium/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (20.35 g/100 g) with omega-3 fatty acids that improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw anchovy is appropriate for Type 2 Diabetes management as it contains no carbohydrate or sugar, has no direct glycaemic impact, and its EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids are associated with improved insulin sensitivity; grilling or steaming is preferred over deep-frying to preserve omega-3 integrity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Rich in EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids; low saturated fat (1.17 g/100 g); cholesterol 60 mg/100 g within acceptable range for a lean oily fish
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw anchovy is cardioprotective due to its high EPA/DHA omega-3 content which reduces triglycerides, lowers inflammatory markers, and supports endothelial function; consumption of 2–3 servings per week of oily fish including anchovy is consistent with AHA cardiovascular guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High EPA/DHA omega-3 content suppresses TNF-alpha and IL-6; calcium (147 mg/100 g) supports bone protection
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw anchovy is beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its anti-inflammatory EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids and bone-protective calcium; only fresh or raw anchovy should be used — canned or salted forms are contraindicated due to very high sodium content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Anchovy, raw refers to fresh, uncooked anchovies (family Engraulidae, commonly Engraulis encrasicolus or related species), small silvery-blue pelagic fish with firm, oily flesh and a distinctively rich, savoury flavour. Raw anchovies are typically 10–20 cm in length and are consumed fresh in Mediterranean and Southeast Asian cuisines — grilled, marinated in citrus (boquerones), steamed, or pan-fried. They are nutritionally dense: 131 kcal/100 g, 20.35 g protein, 4.84 g fat, and zero carbohydrate. The fat profile is dominated by long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are clinically significant for cardiovascular protection, reduction of systemic inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis, and improvement of insulin sensitivity in Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is low at 104 mg/100 g in the raw state — sharply contrasting with canned or salted anchovy products (3000–4000 mg/100 g). Potassium at 383 mg/100 g and calcium at 147 mg/100 g make raw anchovy an excellent DASH-diet-compatible protein source. Iron at 3.25 mg/100 g is notable for a fish. Raw anchovy is gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. It is distinct from processed anchovy products (fish sauce, anchovy paste, canned anchovies) which have entirely different nutritional profiles. In Singapore and Southeast Asia, fresh anchovy is sometimes referred to as ikan bilis in its fresh form, though ikan bilis more commonly refers to the dried and salted preparation used as a condiment or stock base.

How much sodium is in raw anchovy?

What are the omega-3 benefits of anchovy?

How many calories in raw anchovy?

notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — anchovy is a pure animal protein and fat source with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore nutrient composition database. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal-derived food with no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for raw anchovy). Saturated_fat_g (1.17 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.24 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.64 g) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159, Anchovy, raw) as the HPB source does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions; these values are consistent with the total fat of 4.84 g reported by HPB. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Singapore nutrient composition entry for this specific record (cr_id F03010044); a future enrichment pass should incorporate these from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); GI is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable per Veritas protocol for zero-carbohydrate foods. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted and confirms that GI testing is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content.
